GuiStatusExpertModeDisabledCleared: מצב מומחה מושבת. ניקה {0} בחירות מתקדמות מוסתרות.
GuiStatusExpertModeDisabledRestored: מצב מומחה מושבת. שינויים בסיכון גבוה ומתקדמים מוסתרים שוב.
GuiStatusExpertModeEnabled: מצב מומחה מופעל. כעת ניתן לראות שינויים בסיכון גבוה ומתקדמות.
GuiStatusSafeModeDisabledCleared: מצב בטוח מושבת. ניקה {0} בחירות נסתרות.
GuiStatusSafeModeDisabledRestored: מצב בטוח מושבת. התצוגה המקורית שוחזרה.
GuiStatusSafeModeEnabled: מצב בטוח מופעל. טוויקים מסוכנים וקשים להיפוך מוסתרים.
GuiAppsSourceWinGetTip: העדיפו WinGet כאשר שני מקורות החבילה זמינים.
GuiAppsSourceChocolateyTip: העדיפו שוקולד כאשר שני מקורות האריזה זמינים.
GuiAppsSourceStoreTip: אפליקציה זו פותחת את Microsoft Store עבור הפעולה שנבחרה.
GuiAppsSourceDirectTip: אפליקציה זו משתמשת במסלול הורדה ישיר עבור הפעולה שנבחרה.
GuiAppsSourceCommandTip: אפליקציה זו משתמשת במסלול פקודה מותאם אישית עבור הפעולה שנבחרה.
GuiBtnRestoreAllTweaks: שחזר את כל השינויים לברירות המחדל של Windows
GuiMenuHelpDocumentationFailed: לא ניתן לפתוח את ה-README הקופץ. בדוק בספרייה המותקנת עבור README.md.
GuiHelpGettingStartedExpertHelp: השתמש בעזרה לנושאי עיון, יומנים והדרכה לשחזור.
GuiHelpGettingStartedExpertMain: השתמש במדריך התחל במסך הראשי אם אתה רוצה את תהליך העבודה המודרך של ההתקנה.
GuiHelpGettingStartedExpertTabs: אחרת, קפוץ ישירות לכרטיסייה הדרושה לכם וסקור את הבחירה שם.
GuiHelpGettingStartedSafeHelp: השתמש בעזרה כאשר אתה רוצה חומר עזר במקום זרימת ההתקנה המודרכת.
GuiHelpGettingStartedSafeMain: השתמש במדריך התחל במסך הראשי כאשר אתה רוצה את ההליכה הראשונה של הריצה.
GuiHelpGettingStartedSafePreview: השתמש ב-Preview Run לפני החלת משהו כדי שתוכל לסקור את ההשפעה תחילה.
GuiHelpGettingStartedStdHelp: השתמש בעזרה מאוחר יותר כאשר עליך לגלות מחדש את זרימות העבודה הזמינות.
GuiHelpGettingStartedStdMain: השתמש במדריך התחל במסך הראשי עבור זרימת הכניסה המודרכת.
GuiHelpGettingStartedStdPreview: הפעלה מקדימה מאפשרת לך לסקור מה ישתנה לפני שתחיל אותה.
GuiHelpRemoteCliPreview: ניהול מרחוק משתמש כרגע ב-PowerShell Remoting דרך ה-CLI בלבד. ה-GUI עדיין לא מספק חיבור מרחוק או זרימת עבודה של הפעלה.
GuiHelpRemoteExpertRequirements: מוכנות ארגונית עדיין דורשת שכבת חיבור מפורשת, מעקב הפעלה לשימוש חוזר, הקשר יעד גלוי, דיווח על ביצוע לכל מכונה ויומנים מצטברים מרוחקים.
GuiHelpRemoteManagedDevices: יש לבדוק מכשירים מנוהלים, בעבודה, בבית ספר או בדומיין עם צוות הניהול המתאים לפני ריצות מרחוק.
GuiHelpRemoteSafeSingleTarget: התייחס לשימוש מרחוק כזרימת עבודה מעבדתית שנבדקה על ידי מנהל: התחבר באופן מפורש, אמת מכונה אחת תחילה והעדף בדיקות תאימות לקריאה בלבד לפני הפעלת ריצות.
GuiHelpRemoteStandardRequirements: לפני השימוש במיקוד מרחוק, אמת את WinRM, גישת חומת אש, אישורים ומגבלות מדיניות של מכשירים מנוהלים.
GuiImportSettingsBackupDetail: טען את הגיבוי הנוכחי של מצב ההפעלה המשמש לביטול ושחזור.
GuiImportSettingsCustomDetail: דפדף לכל קובץ JSON הגדרות Baseline תואם.
GuiImportSettingsNoLastRun: אין עדיין פרופיל ריצה אחרון זמין.
GuiImportSettingsNoSessionBackup: עדיין אין גיבוי של הפעלה זמין.
GuiImportSettingsOwnDetail: פתח פרופיל הגדרות בסיס מתיקיית ההגדרות {0}.
GuiHistoryViewerSubtitle: סקור, שאילתה וייצא נתוני ריצה היסטוריים.
GuiImportSettingsRecommendedDetail: טען את הפרופיל שנשמר לאחר ש-Baseline סיים את הריצה האחרונה שלו.

GuiPresetMinimalTooltip: נקודת התחלה קטנה עם סיכון נמוך. טוב כשאתה רוצה קו בסיס שמרני מאוד.
GuiPresetBasicTooltip: ברירת מחדל מומלצת לרוב המשתמשים. בוחר תיקון בטוח ברמת סיכון נמוך.
GuiPresetBalancedSafeTooltip: למשתמשים מנוסים. כבה את מצב בטוח כדי להשתמש במאוזן עם ראות מלאה.
GuiPresetAdvancedSafeTooltip: למומחה בלבד. כבה את מצב בטוח והפעל תחילה את מצב מומחה.
GuiPresetBalancedTooltip: לחובבים שמקבלים פשרות מתונות. כולל כוונון רחב יותר מאשר Basic.
GuiPresetAdvancedTooltip: הגדרה מראש של מומחה למשתמשים מנוסים. שינויים בסיכון גבוה עשויים להשפיע על התאימות וההתאוששות. נקודת שחזור מומלצת.
GuiUpdateDialogDescription: גרסה חדשה של Baseline זמינה מ-GitHub. האם אתה רוצה להוריד ולחלץ אותו עכשיו?

TweakWhy_AdminApprovalMode: Controls how User Account Control behaves when an app requests elevated privileges. ×‘×¨×™×¨×ª ×ž×—×“×œ prompts for consent, Never disables UAC prompts. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_AeroShaking: Shaking a window title bar minimizes all other open windows. ×”×©×‘×ª if you find this triggers by accident during normal use.
TweakWhy_AppColorMode: Sets the default color mode that applications use when they respect the system setting: ××¤×œ or ×‘×”×™×¨.
TweakWhy_BSoDStopError: ×”×¦×’s the specific error code on the blue screen of death instead of just the generic sad face emoji.
TweakWhy_BraveDebloat: ×”×©×‘×ªs Brave browser's optional extras: rewards, crypto wallet, VPN, and AI chat. ×“×œ×•×§ly affects these Brave-specific features, not your browsing. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_CABInstallContext: Adds an ×”×ª×§×Ÿ option to the right-click context menu for .cab archive files.
TweakWhy_CheckBoxes: ×”×¦×’s small checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer to make it easier to select multiple items without holding Ctrl.
TweakWhy_ClipboardHistory: Stores a history of copied items so you can paste from previous clips using Win+V. ×”×¤×¢×œ to turn this feature on.
TweakWhy_CompressedFolderNewContext: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Compressed (zipped) Folder option in the right-click New submenu in File Explorer.
TweakWhy_CrossDeviceResume: Lets you resume tasks from other Windows devices on this PC via a notification pop-up. ×”×©×‘×ª if you do not use this cross-device feature.
TweakWhy_DefenderCloud: ×”×¤×¢×œs Microsoft Defender's cloud-based protection for real-time threat lookups using Microsoft's global threat intelligence database. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DefenderTrayIcon: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Windows Security (Defender) icon in the system tray. Hiding it does not disable Defender protection.
TweakWhy_DisableAESCiphers: ×”×©×‘×ªs older weak AES cipher entries in the Windows SCHANNEL security layer. Forces applications to negotiate stronger cipher configurations. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableHashAlgorithms: Configures SCHANNEL hash algorithm settings. ×”×©×‘×ªs MD5 and enables SHA family algorithms for stronger integrity verification. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableIPv6: ×”×©×‘×ªs the IPv6 protocol stack system-wide. ×ž×•×ž×œ×¥ if your network does not use IPv6 to reduce unnecessary attack surface. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableRC2RC4Ciphers: ×”×©×‘×ªs RC2 and RC4 ciphers in SCHANNEL. These algorithms are cryptographically broken and should not be used in any TLS connection. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableSMBv3Compression: Turns off SMB compression to protect against CVE-2020-0796. ×“×œ×•×§ly affects SMB traffic compression, not file access speed. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableTripleDESCipher: ×”×©×‘×ªs Triple DES (3DES) in SCHANNEL. 3DES is vulnerable to SWEET32 birthday attacks and is no longer considered secure. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_EditWithClipchampContext: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Edit with Clipchamp video editor option in the right-click context menu for video files.
TweakWhy_EditWithPaintContext: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Edit with Paint option in the right-click context menu for image files.
TweakWhy_EditWithPhotosContext: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Edit with Photos option in the right-click context menu for image files.
TweakWhy_EnableBiometricsAntiSpoofing: ×”×¤×¢×œs enhanced anti-spoofing for Windows Hello facial recognition. ×“×œ×•×§ly applies if you have compatible biometric hardware.
TweakWhy_EncCompFilesColor: ×”×¦×’s encrypted files in green and compressed files in blue in File Explorer. Makes these file types immediately identifiable.
TweakWhy_EnhPointerPrecision: Mouse acceleration that adjusts cursor speed based on how fast you physically move the mouse. ×”×©×‘×ª for consistent raw mouse input, recommended for gaming.
TweakWhy_ExplorerTitleFullPath: ×”×¦×’s the complete folder path in the File Explorer title bar instead of just the folder name. Useful when working with many folders of the same name.
TweakWhy_F8BootMenu: ×”×¤×¢×œs the legacy F8 key boot menu that lets you select Safe Mode and other startup options during the Windows boot process.
TweakWhy_FileDeleteConfirm: ×”×¦×’s a confirmation dialog before permanently deleting a file. ×”×¤×¢×œ to get a prompt before files are sent to the Recycle Bin.
TweakWhy_FileExtensions: ×”×¦×’s the file extension (e.g. .txt, .exe) after every filename in Explorer. Helps identify what type a file is at a glance.
TweakWhy_FileOperationsDetails: ×”×¦×’s the detailed progress dialog for file operations (copy, move, delete) by default instead of the minimal progress bar.
TweakWhy_Firewall: ×”×¤×¢×œs or disables Windows Defender Firewall across all network profiles. Disabling leaves all inbound ports completely unfiltered. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_FirstLogonAnimation: ×”×¦×’s a brief welcome animation and the Hi screen the first time you sign in after a major Windows update.
TweakWhy_HiddenItems: ×”×¦×’s files and folders that are marked as hidden in File Explorer. Hidden items appear slightly transparent when visible.
TweakWhy_LanguageListAccess: Allows websites to read your Windows language preference list to tailor content. ×”×©×‘×ª to prevent websites from detecting your language settings.
TweakWhy_LanmanWorkstationGuestAuthPolic: Guest authentication for SMB is a known lateral-movement vector disabled by default since Windows 10 1709. Enabling it exposes the system to unauthenticated SMB access. ×“×œ×•×§ly enable for legacy device compatibility.
TweakWhy_LocalSecurityAuthority: Runs the Windows LSA process in protected mode to prevent credential theft by tools like Mimikatz. ×ž×•×ž×œ×¥ for all systems. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_MergeConflicts: ×”×¦×’s a warning when merging folders that contain files with the same name. ×”×©×‘×ª to silently overwrite files without a prompt.
TweakWhy_MostUsedApps: ×”×¦×’s your most frequently launched apps at the top of the Start Menu app list for quick access.
TweakWhy_NaglesAlgorithm: Reduces network buffering so packets are sent immediately. Can lower latency in competitive multiplayer, but may affect connection stability on some networks. ×”×©×‘×ª if you notice connection drops.
TweakWhy_NavPaneAllFolders: ×”×¦×’s all local folders including hidden system folders in the Explorer left navigation pane.
TweakWhy_NavPaneLibraries: ×”×¦×’s the Libraries section (Documents, Music, Pictures, Videos) in the Explorer left navigation pane.
TweakWhy_NetworkDiscovery: ×”×¤×¢×œs this PC to find other devices on the local network and be visible to them. Required for browsing network shares in File Explorer. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_NewOutlook: Controls whether the new Outlook app is available. ×”×©×‘×ª to keep using the classic Mail and Calendar apps instead.
TweakWhy_NumLock: Controls whether the Num Lock key is automatically turned on when Windows starts. ×”×¤×¢×œ to always start with the number pad active.
TweakWhy_PUAppsDetection: ×”×¤×¢×œs Defender to detect and quarantine potentially unwanted applications such as adware, bundleware, and browser hijackers. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_PowerPlan: Sets the Windows power plan. Balanced scales performance based on load. ×’×‘×•×” Performance keeps the CPU at full speed. Ultimate Performance removes all power-saving throttling for maximum throughput. Ultimate Performance may not be available on all systems.
TweakWhy_Powershell7Telemetry: PowerShell 7 sends anonymous usage data to Microsoft by default. Disabling opts out of this telemetry. Linked to the ×”×ª×§×Ÿ/Update PowerShell 7 option.
TweakWhy_PrintCMDContext: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Print option in the right-click context menu for .bat and .cmd script files.
TweakWhy_ProtectClickOnce: ×”×©×‘×ªs Click×“×œ×•×§ce trust prompts for all security zones. Prevents untrusted Click×“×œ×•×§ce applications from prompting for installation. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectFileSystemPerformance: ×”×©×‘×ªs 8.3 short filename generation on NTFS. Improves filesystem performance and removes a legacy compatibility feature rarely needed today.
TweakWhy_ProtectMSOffice: Restricts macros, external content execution, and Outlook trust settings across ×›×‘×•×™ice versions. Reduces document-based attack vectors. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectRPCSurface: ×”×©×‘×ªs RPC-over-TCP for Task Scheduler and the remote Service Control Manager. Reduces exposure for remote management attack paths. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectWinRM: ×”×©×‘×ªs unencrypted WinRM traffic and digest authentication, then restarts the WinRM service. Ensures remote management connections are always encrypted. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_QuickAccessFrequentFolders: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show frequently visited folders. ×”×¡×ª×¨ to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.
TweakWhy_QuickAccessRecentFiles: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show recently opened files. ×”×¡×ª×¨ to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.
TweakWhy_RecentFiles: Controls whether Windows tracks recently accessed files for display in Quick Access and Open dialogs. ×”×©×‘×ª to stop this tracking.
TweakWhy_RecentShortcuts: ×”×¦×’s recently and frequently accessed files and folders as shortcuts in the Explorer Quick Access section.
TweakWhy_RecentlyAddedApps: ×”×¦×’s a list of recently installed apps in the Start Menu. ×”×©×‘×ª to keep the Start Menu app list cleaner.
TweakWhy_RecommendedTroubleshooting: Controls whether Windows automatically runs troubleshooters when problems are detected. ×‘×¨×™×¨×ª ×ž×—×“×œ still asks for your permission before running.
TweakWhy_RecycleBinDeleteConfirmation: ×”×¦×’s a confirmation prompt before moving files to the Recycle Bin. ×”×¤×¢×œ to prevent accidental deletion.
TweakWhy_RegistryBackup: ×”×¤×¢×œs automatic periodic backup of the Windows registry hive files to the RegBack folder inside the Windows directory.
TweakWhy_RestartNotification: ×”×¦×’s a notification in the taskbar when a Windows Update has been installed and requires a restart to complete.
TweakWhy_SMB1: SMBv1 is an outdated and vulnerable file sharing protocol known for EternalBlue exploits. ×”×©×‘×ª unless required for very old network devices. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SMBServer: ×”×¤×¢×œs the SMB file server component that lets other computers on the network access shared folders and printers on this PC. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SetWindowsFirewallLogging: ×”×¤×¢×œs logging of dropped and allowed connections to the Windows Firewall log file so you can audit network activity.
TweakWhy_SharingWizard: ×”×¤×¢×œs the simplified file sharing wizard when you right-click a file or folder and choose to share it.
TweakWhy_ShortcutArrow: ×”×¦×’s or hides the small arrow overlay icon on shortcut files to indicate they are shortcuts and not the original files.
TweakWhy_SigninInfo: Uses your sign-in credentials to automatically finish setting up your profile after a Windows update. ×”×©×‘×ª for more privacy.
TweakWhy_SleepTimeout: Controls when the display turns off and when the PC enters sleep mode after inactivity. ×”×©×‘×ª to prevent automatic sleep.
TweakWhy_SnapAssist: When you snap a window to one side, Windows suggests other open windows to fill the remaining space. ×”×©×‘×ª to snap without suggestions.
TweakWhy_StartRecommendedSection: ×”×¦×’s or hides the ×ž×•×ž×œ×¥ section at the bottom of the Start Menu. ×“×œ×•×§ Enterprise and Education editions this can be hidden via policy.
TweakWhy_StickyShift: Pressing Shift five times quickly triggers the Sticky Keys accessibility dialog. ×”×©×‘×ª to prevent this from interrupting your work.
TweakWhy_SuperHiddenFiles: ×”×¦×’s protected Windows system files in File Explorer. These are hidden by default to prevent accidental modification or deletion.
TweakWhy_Superfetch: SysMain preloads frequently used apps into RAM to speed up launch times. ×“×œ×•×§ SSDs it provides little benefit and can usually be safely disabled. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SuspendAirstrikeAttack: ×”×¡×ª×¨s the network selection UI from the Windows lock screen. Prevents changing network settings before signing in. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SyncNotifications: OneDrive and other sync providers can show promotional notifications inside Explorer. ×”×©×‘×ª to hide these banners.
TweakWhy_ThisPC: ×”×¦×’s or hides the This PC icon on your desktop for quick access to drives and devices.
TweakWhy_Thumbnails: ×”×¦×’s image and video previews as thumbnails in Explorer instead of generic file type icons. Disabling shows icons only.
TweakWhy_TrayIcons: ×”×¦×’s all notification area icons in the system tray instead of hiding overflow icons behind the expand arrow.
TweakWhy_UpdateKeyExchanges: ×”×¤×¢×œs Diffie-Hellman, ECDH, and PKCS key exchange algorithms in SCHANNEL. Ensures forward-secure key exchange is available for TLS connections. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateMSProducts: When enabled, Windows Update also checks for updates to other Microsoft products like ×›×‘×•×™ice. Disabling limits updates to Windows only.
TweakWhy_UseStoreOpenWith: ×”×¦×’s or hides the Look for an app in the Microsoft Store option that appears in the Open With dialog for unassociated file types.
TweakWhy_VerboseStatus: ×”×¦×’s detailed status messages during Windows startup and shutdown instead of just the generic loading spinner.
TweakWhy_WebLangList: Syncs your preferred language list to a Microsoft server so web content can match your preferences. ×”×©×‘×ª for more privacy.
TweakWhy_WhatsNewInWindows: ×”×¦×’s banners and prompts highlighting new Windows features after updates. ×”×©×‘×ª to suppress these notifications.
TweakWhy_WindowsColorMode: Sets whether the Windows shell (taskbar, Start Menu, action center) uses ××¤×œ or ×‘×”×™×¨ mode.
TweakWhy_WindowsWelcomeExperience: ×”×¦×’s a full-screen welcome page after major Windows updates highlighting new features. ×”×©×‘×ª to skip this screen.
TweakWhy_XboxGameTips: ×”×¦×’s onboarding tips and hints inside the Xbox Game Bar overlay. ×”×©×‘×ª if you are already familiar with Game Bar.
